## 3. Priorities & Struggles

**What Julien said matters most (his own words, highest trust):**

> "It's not about the games I develop today. It's not about any of my dozens of projects. It's about what I'm going to start doing seriously in 6 months. Right now, it's only about understanding what's happening, understanding what's worth pursuing and what is not. I have no idea. That's why I'm trying so many things. I need to be at the edge."

This is the operating frame. The consolidation isn't laziness — it's the natural exhale after a sprint of trying many things. He's in a sensing mode, not a building mode.

**The core struggle:** He has built a lot (blog pipeline, Grassroot Hoppers, dancing/guitar skills, business autonomy systems, Values rewrite, March action plan) but hasn't yet found the thing that pulls him seriously for the next chapter. The 6-month clock is his own framing — something starts then, and he doesn't know what it is yet.

**What he's not doing:** No journaling. No commits. No visible reflection output. The pause is clean but opaque — there's no written evidence of what he's processing internally.
